pi_items.verified_value: GIMP 2.10 actually has: Luminance, Luma, Lightness, Average, Value
GIMP 2.10 calls it 'Luminance' or 'Luma', not 'Luminosity'. Uses formula: (0.22×R) + (0.72×G) + (0.06×B)
pi_items.source: GIMP 2.10 official documentation; README.md line 24 uses incorrect name

pi_items.metadata_value: Mean absolute difference between R,G,B channels ≤ 2.0 units per pixel
At least 95% of pixels have saturation values below 0.05
Standard deviation of grayscale values ≥ 60% of original luminosity standard deviation
Standard deviation of brightness values ≥ 15 (avoiding flat gray)
75% (requires at least 3 out of 4 verification criteria)

pi_items.verified_value: PIL (Image module) and numpy imported in verifier.py lines 9-10
pi_items.source: verifier.py imports; setup_desaturate.sh line 8 installs python3-pil python3-numpy

pi_items.verified_value: export_desaturate.sh automates export with xdotool, results in /home/ga/Desktop/desaturated_bw.jpg
pi_items.source: export_desaturate.sh line 39-41 checks for .jpg file; verifier.py line 137 checks possible_results

privileged_info_summary: The desaturate_bw@1 task uses real images downloaded from Unsplash (URLs verified as valid and accessible). Primary image sources are an Unsplash flower photo (111.7KB) and forest photo (212.2KB), with an ImageMagick-generated synthetic fallback if downloads fail. The task requires GIMP 2.10 with desaturation functions (Luminance/Luma, Lightness, Average, Value). Verification uses PIL and numpy to check: RGB channel equality (tolerance ≤2.0), pixel saturation (<0.05 for ≥95% of pixels), detail preservation (≥60% of original luminosity standard deviation), and meaningful tonal range (standard deviation ≥15). Pass threshold is 75% (≥3 of 4 criteria). Minor documentation inaccuracy: README calls the standard desaturation method 'Luminosity' but GIMP 2.10 documentation calls it 'Luminance' or 'Luma'.
